hypothesis: Applying equivariance-inducing regularization (e.g., equigrad) to string- and language-based chemical models during multimodal joint pretraining will cause their embeddings to converge onto the same low-dimensional equivariant manifold as high-performing equivariant MLIPs, producing significantly higher CKNNA and IDCor with equivariant MLIPs on both in-distribution (OMat24) and out-of-distribution (OMol25) evaluation sets than identical models trained without such regularization.
